title: Phanera excurrens
intro: Phanera excurrens, also known as the climbing staff tree, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Caesalpiniaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as arthritis, diabetes, and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Phanera excurrens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Phanera excurrens is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Phanera excurrens is classified under the family Apocynaceae. This classification places Phanera excurrens within the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants. The Apocynaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Phanera excurrens being a member of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae. Phanera excurrens is a genus of flowering plants native to Asia. The exact classification of Phanera excurrens within the family Apocynaceae may require further research for precise details.
